channel.title: grpc on IT notes
channel.description: Recent content in grpc on IT notes

item.title: grpc
item.description: What is gRPC? A remote procedure call (RPC) framework that by default uses protocol buffers as the Interface Definition Language (IDL). One of the initial benefits is the ability to be able to define and codify your service requirements via a .proto file. It uses HTTP/2 for transport but is not yet available for use within the browser, therefore currently mainly in use for Inter-Process Communication (IPC) within microservices. The workflow to create a gRPC service Create the service definition and payload structure in the .
